businessSummary.oneLiner: Government and commercial AI data analytics platform leader with Gotham (defense), Foundry (enterprise), and AIP (AI Platform)
businessSummary.description: Palantir builds AI data analytics platforms for government and enterprise clients. FY2025 revenue hit $4.48B (+56%), with US commercial revenue surpassing $500M. The Navy ShipOS project now includes submarine production AI. Stock gained 1,860% over three years but experienced 10+ drawdowns of 20%+.

keyMetrics.name: P/E (TTM)
keyMetrics.interpretation: 10x S&P 500 avg (22x). Still extreme but war-driven defense AI spending may justify the premium

keyMetrics.name: Forward P/E
keyMetrics.interpretation: Based on FY2026 EPS $1.34. War premium sustaining elevated multiples

keyMetrics.name: P/S Ratio
keyMetrics.interpretation: Extreme vs SaaS avg 10-15x but justified by 60%+ growth acceleration

keyMetrics.name: Operating Margin
keyMetrics.interpretation: Top-tier among SaaS. Growth and profitability simultaneously

growthDrivers.title: Iran War $200B Budget: Structural Defense AI Demand Surge
growthDrivers.description: Pentagon requested $200B for the Iran war. War expansion drives explosive demand for military AI, intelligence analysis, and logistics automation. Gotham, Maven, ShipOS all direct beneficiaries.

growthDrivers.title: Moder AI Mortgage Partnership: Financial Services Entry
growthDrivers.description: Freedom Mortgage as first pilot for AI mortgage operations platform. Beyond defense, expanding into finance.

growthDrivers.title: Mizuho: 'No Comparable in Software' Outperform $195
growthDrivers.description: Post-management meeting, Mizuho reaffirmed Outperform. Called Palantir unparalleled in software.

growthDrivers.title: UBS $200 Raise + Wedbush $230 Maintained
growthDrivers.description: UBS raised from $180 to $200 (+33%). Wedbush $230 Buy maintained.

growthDrivers.title: Navy ShipOS $448M: AI for Submarine Production
growthDrivers.description: Keel joined Palantir's Navy ShipOS project. AI applied to submarine production lines.

growthDrivers.title: $11.2B Revenue Backlog + $10B Army Contract
growthDrivers.description: $10B Army contract reshaping defense software market. Multi-year revenue visibility secured.

competitiveAdvantage.summary: 20+ year government and defense AI track record. Security clearances create insurmountable entry barriers. ShipOS expands AI across military production.

moats.description: Highest-level DoD and intelligence clearances. Competitors cannot enter.
AI deployed in submarine production and naval shipyards. Beyond battlefield to military manufacturing.
Sovereign AI OS + NVIDIA hardware ecosystem. Setting government AI infrastructure standards globally.

competitors.name: Microsoft (Azure AI)
competitors.detail: Cloud AI platform. General approach vs Palantir's specialized analytics

competitors.name: C3.ai
competitors.detail: Enterprise AI. Lags Palantir in scale and track record

recentNews.headline: Iran Ground War: Pentagon Requests $200B War Budget
recentNews.significance: War expansion means direct benefit for defense AI. Gotham, Maven, ShipOS all in scope. PLTR fell 3.25% with broader market.

recentNews.headline: Motley Fool: 'Market Is Still Missing Something on Palantir'
recentNews.significance: Analysis argues PLTR looks expensive but growth-adjusted gap is significant. Rule of 40 score of 127% is rare.

recentNews.headline: Mizuho Reaffirms Outperform $195: 'No Comparable'
recentNews.significance: Post-management meeting bullish call. Focus on AIP pilot-to-paid conversion rates.

recentNews.headline: Moder AI Mortgage Platform Partnership Announced
recentNews.significance: Freedom Mortgage pilot. Financial services entry validates commercial expansion beyond defense.

recentNews.headline: Pentagon Pushing to Remove Anthropic Claude from Maven
recentNews.significance: Six-month timeline to replace Claude in $1B+ Maven contract. Short-term disruption risk.

analystOpinions.notableComment: UBS $200 (+33%), Wedbush $230 (+53%), Mizuho $195 reaffirmed. 16 of 28 Buy. Iran war $200B budget could structurally boost defense AI demand.

risks.title: Extreme Valuation: 221x P/E, 77x P/S
risks.description: 10x S&P 500 average. FCF yield under 1%. Even war tailwinds may not justify this premium if recession hits.

risks.title: Iran War Prolongation: Recession vs Defense Spending
risks.description: $200B war budget helps defense, but Hormuz blockade and oil surge could trigger recession that crushes commercial IT spending. Double-edged sword for Palantir as commercial revenue grows.

risks.title: Insider Selling Continues
risks.description: CEO Alex Karp $6.6M, Stephen Cohen $43.7M in recent sales. Management actively taking profits.

risks.title: Swiss Lawsuit + NHS Controversy: European Risk
risks.description: Guardian labeled Swiss magazine lawsuit as 'intimidation campaign'. NHS hospital group chair resigned over Palantir advisory role.

risks.title: Stock Dilution
risks.description: ~5% annual dilution from SBC. Erodes long-term returns.

risks.title: Pentagon Anthropic Claude Removal Disruption
risks.description: Maven AI engine replacement creates cost and timeline uncertainty.

buyReasons.title: Iran War $200B: Structural Defense AI Demand Increase
buyReasons.rationale: War expansion directly increases military AI, intelligence, and logistics automation demand. Palantir is the most direct beneficiary.

buyReasons.title: Defensive Relative Strength: Holding Above 200-Day MA
buyReasons.rationale: Staying above 200-day ($127) while S&P breaks below its own. Defense theme provides downside protection.

buyReasons.title: Moder Mortgage + Triple Analyst Bullishness
buyReasons.rationale: Financial services TAM expansion. UBS/Wedbush/Mizuho all bullish simultaneously.

buyReasons.title: Rule of 40 Score 127%: Growth + Profitability Rarity
buyReasons.rationale: 60% growth and 31% operating margin simultaneously achieved by very few companies.

overallOpinion: The Iran war expanded to ground operations with Pentagon requesting $200B. War expansion is the most direct positive catalyst for defense AI. Palantir's Gotham, Maven, and ShipOS are all beneficiaries.

The broader market fell 1.5%, but Palantir maintained its position above the 200-day MA ($127), showing relative strength. The Moder mortgage partnership and triple analyst bullishness from Mizuho, UBS, and Wedbush keep the growth story intact.
Below the 50-day ($157) but above the 200-day. P/E 221x is daunting, but if war structurally increases defense AI spending, the premium may be justified. Buy on pullbacks.

investmentVerdict.summary: Iran war's $200B budget is a structural tailwind for Palantir. Gotham, Maven, ShipOS all directly benefit. P/E 221x is challenging, but Rule of 40 at 127%, 60% growth acceleration, and $11.2B backlog provide support. European risks and insider selling require monitoring, but US defense and commercial expansion remain solid. Buy on dips.

empathy.hook: PLTR holders, another -3% day. But the defense thesis just got stronger
empathy.text:
$PLTR $150.68 (-3.25%)

Another red day. The market is crashing on Iran war fears and PLTR fell with it. If you've held through ten 20%+ drops in three years, you know the drill.

But this time, the war is directly boosting your thesis. $200B Pentagon budget means more Gotham, more Maven, more ShipOS. Mizuho, UBS, Wedbush all bullish.

Stay patient. DCA on dips. Buy. Target $196 (+30%).
